story.text: As the loop's owner, I want the remaining fixed-verdict mock gates (handoff-gate, value-confirm) de-mocked under admitted, rung-checked backings — inference or deterministic per the question each gate actually asks — and 'the five gates' enumerated once on the record, so the ambient hook's mock temperature reads 0/5 and every gate carries at least one non-accept verdict in its history. A gate that has never refused is a mock with a bigger bill (report 0004); first-light proved the value gate can say no (rcp.3b62807d5f6a), and this atom carries that proof across the rest of the pipeline.

_note: v1 re-versions v0 so the now-real gates judge the work the mocks rubber-stamped (v0 reached value_confirmed entirely through fixed-verdict mocks for placement/handoff/value-confirm — a false confirm). The claim is unchanged because the work is unchanged: mock temperature 0/5 and every gate carrying a non-accept verdict in its history. As of this version that is NOT delivered — handoff-gate is still mock, and no gate yet carries a non-accept verdict on the live trunk log — so a real value-confirm must read this as `missed`, the honest report that intention and outcome diverge. That refusal is the point: it is value-confirm's first light and the proof the gate is a capable judge of delivery, not a turnstile.
